explanation: This command bypasses the specific string match for '::$index_allocation' by using a trailing dot and the Win32 Namespace prefix ('\\?\'). This creates a 'Reserved Name' folder that is inaccessible via standard Windows Explorer and many CLI tools, achieving a similar hidden/restricted directory effect without triggering the NTFS stream-based detection pattern.

This bypass leverages environment variable expansion. Since the Sigma rule looks for the literal string '::$index_allocation' in the process command line, splitting the string into variables prevents the static pattern match while still resulting in the creation of the NTFS stream directory when executed.

The Sigma rule is case-sensitive or generally targets the standard lowercase/mixed-case string. Using '::$INDEX_ALLOCATION' (all caps) may bypass simple string-matching filters in SIEMs or EDRs that are not configured for case-insensitive regex, while NTFS remains case-insensitive and will process the stream correctly.
